title: Off-Road to Uchar Waterfall
description: By Taxi + hiking 8 Kms

title: Maral Deer Farm & Antler Baths
description: May-Jun only. A Maral Deer Farm and Antler Bath excursion is a popular eco-wellness and cultural tour where visitors observe Siberian red deer (marals) in their natural habitat and take part in traditional therapeutic "panto-baths". These restorative treatments utilize extracts from unossified velvet antlers to boost immunity and promote vitality

title: Geyser Lake & Mars Rocks
description:
Geyser Lake (Ozheo-Kol): Located 5 km from the village of Aktash, this thermal lake is famous for its vibrant azure color and underwater springs that push bubbling clay to the surface, creating constantly shifting patterns. 
Altai Mars (Kyzyl-Chin): Often compared to the surface of Mars, this area features striking, colorful clay mountains. Formed by oxidized minerals, the landscape ranges from deep reds and oranges to yellow and purple hues Shamanic Ritual & Throat Singing: Visit real Altai shaman.

title: Rafting on Katun River
description: Grade 3-4 rapids. 1-day to 7-day routes. Turquoise water from glaciers. Combine with camping.

title: Horse Trek to Karakol Lakes
description: 3-day horse ride through cedar taiga to chain of 7 alpine lakes at 2,000m. Stay in yurts. Shamanic energy places.

title: Paratunka Hot Springs & Vilyuchinsky Volcano
description: Relax in natural thermal pools in Paratunka valley, 1hr from city. Day hike to Vilyuchinsky waterfall & extrusions. ATV tours, dog sledding in winter. Best acclimatization day.

title: Avacha Bay Boat Trip + Starichkov Island
description: 6-hr boat cruise from Petropavlovsk. See “Three Brothers” rocks, black sand beaches, sea lions. Starichkov Island: bird colonies – puffins, cormorants, sea eagles. Fishing for halibut & crab + fresh lunch onboard. Whale watching Jun-Sep.

title: Mutnovsky & Gorely Volcanoes
description: Day tour by 6x4 vehicle to “Small Valley of Geysers” at Mutnovsky. Hike into crater with fumaroles, boiling mud, glacier. Gorely: Climb to crater with 3 lakes incl. acid turquoise lake. No heli needed – best land-based volcano day trip.

title: Kuril Lake Bear Watching
description: Helicopter to Kuril Lake in South Kamchatka Sanctuary. Jul-Aug: Hundreds of brown bears gather to catch sockeye salmon. Safest bear viewing in world from elevated platforms. Combine with volcano flyover. Photographer’s paradise.

title: Valley of Geysers & Uzon Caldera Helicopter Tour
description: 6-hr MI-8 helicopter excursion. Fly over active volcanoes Karymsky & Maly Semyachik. Land in Valley of Geysers for 1.5hr walk among erupting geysers. Second landing in Uzon Caldera – acid lakes, mud pots, fumaroles. Lunch at hot spring. Must-do #1

title: Petrozavodsk – Capital of Karelia
location: St. Petersburg and the Arctic Circle
description: By Taxi

title: Kivach Waterfall & Karelian Nature
location: St. Petersburg and the Arctic Circle
description: One of Europe’s highest plain waterfalls, 10.7m. Located in Kivach Nature Reserve with arboretum & museum. Combine with Girvas paleovolcano and Sampo Mountain for epic views.

title: Valaam Monastery on Lake Ladoga
location: St. Petersburg and the Arctic Circle
description: 14th-century Orthodox monastery on Valaam Archipelago in Lake Ladoga – Europe’s largest lake. Spiritual center with golden domes, sketes, and pristine nature. Day trip by hydrofoil from Sortavala May-Sep.

title: Ruskeala Mountain Park & Marble Canyon
location: St. Petersburg and the Arctic Circle
description: Flooded marble quarry with turquoise water surrounded by marble cliffs. Summer: Boat rides in canyon, zip-line, diving. Winter: Ice sculptures & artistic lighting in canyon. Retro steam train “Ruskeala Express” from Sortavala.

title: Kizhi Island & Kizhi Pogost
location: St. Petersburg and the Arctic Circle
description: Open-air museum on Lake Onega: Home to 22-dome Transfiguration Church built in 1714 without a single nail. 80+ historic wooden buildings: chapels, windmills, peasant houses. Reachable by hydrofoil from Petrozavodsk May-Oct or hovercraft in winter.

title: Svetlogorsk & Zelenogradsk Resort Towns
description: Svetlogorsk: “Russian Jurmala”. 19th-century German resort town Rauschen. Funicular to beach, water tower, promenade.Zelenogradsk: “Cat Capital”. Beach town with German villas, cat sculptures, and longest promenade in Russia.

title: Amber Museum & Yantarny Amber Mine
description: Housed in a 19th-century fortified tower. World’s largest amber piece: 4.2 kg. In Yantarny village, tour the world’s only open-pit amber mine. Workshop to make your own amber souvenir.

title: Curonian Spit National Park UNESCO Site
description: 98 km narrow sand dune peninsula shared with Lithuania. Highlights: Dancing Forest with twisted pine trees, Epha Height dune for Baltic Sea + Curonian Lagoon views, wide sandy beaches. Ornithological station on major bird migration route.

title: Königsberg Cathedral & Kant Island
description: 14th-century Gothic cathedral on an island in the Pregolya River. Tomb of philosopher Immanuel Kant. Hosts organ concerts daily. Surrounded by the Fish Village – reconstructed medieval-style quarter with cafes and amber shops.

title: Listvyanka Village Tour
description: “Gateway to Baikal”70 km from Irkutsk. Best first stop for Baikal views. Visit Baikal Museum, Chersky Stone viewpoint, St. Nicholas Church. Fish market for local Omul fish. Starting point for summer cruises.

title: Olkhon Island Tour
description: “The Heart of Baikal”Largest island on Baikal and sacred to Buryat people. Key sites: Shaman Rock/Cape Burkhan, Khoboy Cape, sandy beaches at Khuzhir. Best place for sunsets, stargazing, and understanding local culture. No paved roads – real adventure.

title: Buryat Cultural Immersion Tour
description: Your best option is an excursion to Ust-Orda, the heart of the Ust-Orda Buryat Okrug, located about 70 km (1.5 hours) outside Irkutsk. Day tours include authentic Buryat cuisine, traditional folklore performances, wrestling demonstrations, and visits with a local shaman.

title: Winter Ice Tour
description:
Walk, skate, or drive on transparent ice. Visit ice caves on Olkhon Island. Ride a hovercraft over frozen Baikal. Try “Baikal Kiss” cocktail served on ice.
Take a fantastic dogsled tour, Feel the speed and adrenaline rush during a snowmobile ride over the frozen surface of the Baikal. Enjoy Baikal Ice yachting, Experience a hovercraft tour (an air cushion vehicle) around the lake Baikal.

title: Rukavishnikov Estate Museum
description: A beautifully preserved 19th-century merchant’s mansion showcasing the luxurious life of Russian nobility. Rich interiors, art collections, and historical exhibits. Today this building hosts one of the branches of Nizhny Novgorod State Museum Reserve of History and Architecture.

title: Nizhny Novgorod Cable Car
description:
Europe’s longest cable car over water, spanning 3.6 km across the Volga to the town of Bor. 12-minute ride with breathtaking views of the river and city skyline.
Operating mode: Monday to Thursday 6:45 to 21:00 & Friday - Saturday 6:45 to 22:00

title: Bolshaya Pokrovskaya Street
description: The city’s main pedestrian street. Lined with 19th-century merchant mansions, street sculptures, cafes, and souvenir shops. Similar to Moscow’s Arbat Street but calmer.

title: Chkalov Stairs
description: Russia’s longest staircase with 560+ steps, built in the shape of a figure "8". The best viewpoint in the city, especially at sunset. Named after Soviet pilot Valery Chkalov.

title: Nizhny Novgorod Kremlin
description: A 16th-century red-brick fortress overlooking the Volga. Walk the ramparts for panoramic views of the river confluence. Houses several museums and the eternal flame.

title: Eco Tour to Matsesta Valley
description: Visit the world's northernmost tea plantations and a working eco-farm nestled inside Sochi National Park. Learn the fascinating process of tea production from leaf to cup, taste organic products with freshly-made pancakes and honey, and capture stunning photographs amidst the lush sub-tropical landscape.

title: "SnowBus" Safari
description: Ride a powerful SnowBus through the legendary Khibiny mountain range, taking in the majestic peaks of Poachvumchorr, Takhtarvumchorr, and Vudyavrchorr. The tour includes 3 specially chosen stops at the most picturesque viewpoints — perfect for photography and unforgettable Arctic memories.

title: "Feel Yourself Russian" Folklore Show
description: An unforgettable two-hour folk performance at the Nikolaevski Palace — built by Tsar Nicholas I in 1861. Experience the soul of Russia through vibrant folk songs and traditional regional dances from across the country, ranked among St. Petersburg's top visitor attractions.

title: Shuvalovka Village
description: Step back to 1714 in this beautifully recreated traditional Northern Russian settlement of authentic log cabins. Year-round festivals, sports tournaments, and folk fairs bring history to life. In winter, enjoy ice skating, sledding, horse-drawn sleigh rides, and horseback riding.

title: Katherine's Palace & The Amber Room
description: Built over the 18th–19th centuries, this 325-metre decorated palace dazzles with gilded sculptures and opulent interiors. At its heart lies the legendary Amber Room — considered a masterpiece of 18th-century jewellery art.

title: Peterhof Park & Palace
description: Often called the "Russian Versailles," this world-famous palace and park complex on the Gulf of Finland shore attracts millions of visitors every year. The Grand Palace's magnificent architecture and spectacular fountain cascades have inspired poets and artists for centuries.

title: Boat Ride on Neva River
description: Glide along the network of rivers and canals that weave through the city's distinct districts. From the water, the magnificent architecture of Saint Petersburg reveals itself in a completely new light — great vistas of the city unfold one after another.

title: Cathedral of the Savior on Spilled Blood
description: Built on the exact spot where Emperor Alexander II was mortally wounded in 1881. A breathtaking masterpiece of Late Russian Style architecture adorned with 7,500 square metres of intricate mosaics depicting biblical scenes.

title: Peter & Paul Fortress
description: Explore the island fortress where Saint Petersburg was born. Home to the city's oldest cathedral dedicated to SS Peter and Paul — this historic site has served as the burial place of nearly all Russian rulers and their family members since the 18th century.

title: State Hermitage Museum
description: The second largest museum in the world (after the Louvre in Paris). Founded by Catherine the Great in 1764 and housed in the spectacular former royal residence, its vast collections span centuries of human civilisation.

title: City Tour
description: Discover the greatest landmarks of the Venice of the North — the grand Palace Square, the iconic Winter Palace and State Hermitage Museum, the imposing Peter and Paul Fortress, the famous Bronze Horseman monument, and the magnificent Kazan Cathedral.

title: Waterfalls
description: Trek to 33 magnificent waterfalls cascading crystal-clear mountain water through rare and ancient plants. Seventeen falls are easily accessible, with the tallest reaching nearly 11 metres. Legend has it each waterfall marks a giant's footprint — an awe-inspiring natural spectacle.

title: City Tour
description: Take in sweeping panoramic views of Sochi from atop Mount Akhun and the Ferris Wheel at 701 metres elevation. Explore the vibrant Riviera park and the historic Sea Port, then round off the day with tastings of the finest local wine, honey, and artisan cheese.

title: Roza Khutor & Krasnaya Polyana
description: Journey into the stunning Caucasus Mountains to explore Krasnaya Polyana — breathtaking natural panoramas, authentic Caucasian cuisine, a natural narzan mineral spring, and three world-class ski resorts. Relive the legacy of the 2014 Winter Olympics and end the day at the spectacular fountain shows in Olympic Park.

title: Boat Trip
description: Set sail on a luxury yacht along the Black Sea coast, with the chance to swim in the open sea and spot playful dolphins. Equipped with blankets and pillows for comfort, the yacht can also be arranged for onboard dining or a romantic sunset cruise.

title: Horse Riding
description: Ride through the scenic outskirts of Sochi on gentle horses, taking in breathtaking views of Kudepsta, the ancient Yewsamshite grove with its rare relict trees, mountain rivers, the dramatic River Canyon, Kudepsta Gorge, and an observation deck over the Olympic Park. Children aged 4+ welcome.

title: All Religions Cathedral
description: A truly unique architectural marvel — a single complex uniting symbols of the world's great faiths under one roof. Explore the Orthodox and Catholic churches, the mosque, synagogue, Buddhist and Krishna temples, and a pagoda, all coexisting in perfect harmony.

title: Downtown Walking Tour
description: Walk one of the oldest streets in the city and meander through the serene Black Lake park. Discover the interactive "City Panorama" museum, visit historic cathedral sites, and stroll through the famous university campus with its landmark astronomical observatory.

title: Ecotourism Park "Wild Farm"
description: Escape into nature at this magnificent 400-hectare natural reserve just 130 km from Kazan. Home to sika deer and marals roaming freely in their natural environment, the park offers a truly peaceful retreat into the forest — a perfect contrast to the city's rich cultural heritage.

title: Gastronomic Tour
description: Savour the rich flavours of Tatar national cuisine — join a master class on preparing traditional pastries and sweets, complete with tastings. Visit the famous Chak-Chak Museum to discover the story of Tatarstan's beloved national treat over a warm cup of tea.

title: Kazan Kremlin Tour
description: A walking tour through one of Russia's UNESCO-listed fortresses. Marvel at the stunning Kul Sharif Mosque and the ancient Cathedral of the Annunciation, hear the legend of the leaning Syuyumbike Tower, and enjoy sweeping panoramic views from the Kremlin observation deck.

title: City Tour
description: Explore the historical heart of the capital of Tatarstan — wander through the Old Tatar settlement with its colourful houses and souvenir shops, stroll along the shores of Lake Kaban, and walk down Kremlevskaya Street where the historic building of Kazan University stands proud.

title: Flight in Weightlessness
description: Board the special IL-76 MDK plane-laboratory used to train cosmonauts and experience true weightlessness. The aircraft follows Kepler's Parabola trajectory, giving you 10 weightless regimes of 25–28 seconds each — one of the most extraordinary experiences available to civilians anywhere in the world.

title: Military Tour
description: Experience the thrill of riding tanks at former USSR military bases and try your hand at shooting from the famous AK-47 Kalashnikov assault rifle or the classic Makarov pistol — an utterly unforgettable adventure.

title: Bar Tour
description: The megapolis never sleeps — enjoy Moscow's legendary nightlife by visiting the best bars and clubs the city has to offer. A qualified and knowledgeable guide accompanies you throughout the night, ensuring an authentic, safe, and memorable experience. Available Friday to Sunday only.

title: Cruise by Radisson Royal
description: Enjoy your time aboard the luxurious Radisson Royal yacht, sailing the Moscow River to the sound of beautiful music while admiring magical city views from the water. Optional restaurant food and beverages are available on board — ideal for a romantic evening or a sophisticated group experience.

title: Moscow Metro & The Old Arbat Street
description: Moscow's metro is recognised as the most beautiful and safest in the world. Opened in 1935, it is also one of the oldest metro systems on Earth. Ride through its spectacular stations — each a masterpiece of Soviet architecture — then stroll the famous Old Arbat Street.

title: Traces of the Soviet Empire
description: Explore the splendour and contrasts of the world's first communist country. Visit the grand Stalinist architecture of VDNH park and dive into the extraordinary history of space exploration at the Museum of Cosmonautics — a fascinating journey through an era that changed the world forever.

title: The Kremlin
description: The oldest and only functioning fortress in Europe — built in the 15th century and still at the heart of Russian power today. Explore the magnificent fortress architecture, the Russian President's residency, the oldest cathedrals, the Patriarch's Palace, and the legendary Tsar-Bell and Tsar-Cannon.

title: "Secrets of the City" Walking Tour
description: Dive into the old streets of the megapolis and feel as though you've been transported back a couple of centuries. Explore Red Square, Tverskaya Street, Manege Square, the ancient Nikolskaya, Varvarka & Ilinka streets, the charming St. Barbara's Church, and the modern Zaryadye park.

title: City Tour
description: Get acquainted with Moscow in just 4 hours — see all the most iconic sights and learn about the history, local way of life, and traditions. Highlights include Red Square, St. Basil's Cathedral, GUM mall, Christ the Savior Cathedral, Moscow City Business Center, Park of Victory, Sparrow Hills observation platform, and the legendary Seven Sisters.

title: Aurora Hunting Tour (3-hrs Program)
description: As night falls over the Arctic, your expert guide leads you away from the city lights to the best Northern Lights viewing spots around Murmansk. Watch the sky come alive with the dancing colours of the Aurora Borealis, and capture stunning photographs with professional guidance.

title: Lovozero Husky Park (3-hrs Program)
description: Venture deep into the tundra on snowmobiles to discover the legendary Lovozero Husky Park. Meet the resident sled dogs, feed reindeer, and enjoy both dog-sled and reindeer-sled rides. Your guide shares fascinating tales of Arctic life, and the program concludes with a warm, satisfying lunch.

title: Husky Sleds Riding
description: Let adorable Siberian Huskies whisk you through snowy Arctic landscapes on a classic dog sled ride. Enjoy the warm and friendly atmosphere of the husky farm, gather around the tea table, hear fascinating stories about the dogs, and create memories you'll treasure for a lifetime.

title: Kirovsk City & "Snow Village"
description: Step inside a breathtaking handmade ice museum with 40 halls crafted by Russian, Norwegian, and Finnish artists — theme changes each season. Optional extras include mountain climbing, ski rentals, exhilarating husky sledding, and SnowBus rides through the Khibiny mountains.

title: Snowmobile Safari
description: Race across the snow-laden Arctic tundra on a powerful snowmobile, weaving through dense forested terrain with agility and speed. Soak in the magnificent views of the endless snow-covered hills stretching to the horizon — an experience that truly captures the spirit of Murmansk's wild north.

title: Saami Village
description: Immerse yourself in the ancient culture of the indigenous Sami people through traditional games, reindeer encounters, and thrilling banana snowmobile rides. Walk the Sacred Idols pathway and make a wish with six coins — a timeless ritual. Includes authentic meals and traditional Arctic beverages.

title: Excursion to Teriberka Village
description: Discover the rich history of this remote Arctic village on the shores of the Barents Sea. Marvel at dramatic coastal vistas, abandoned vessels, the famous Dragon Eggs beach, and breathtaking waterfalls. Stack stones to make a wish — a cherished local tradition. Winter visits add sled and snowmobile rides to the adventure.

title: Suzdal Town Tour
description: Visit the Kremlin of Suzdal (12th century) and the Museum of Wooden Architecture, featuring wooden houses, a windmill, and even a functioning Orthodox church from the 18–19th centuries — Russia's best-preserved medieval town.

title: Vladimir City Tour
description: Tour begins at the iconic Golden Gates (1158), continues to the Cathedral of St. Demetrius (pre-Mongol period, now a museum), and concludes at the Assumption Cathedral — a magnificent 12th-century Orthodox temple.

title: Saint Trinity Sergius Lavra. Sergiev Posad
description: One of the oldest monasteries in Russia (founded in 1337) and the most revered one. You will be impressed by the medieval Russian architecture: gorgeous cathedrals with gold-covered domes and the most beautiful 88-metre-high belfry in Russia.

title: Master-class in Matryoshka factory. Sergiev Posad
description: If you visit Sergiev Posad – the Motherland of Matryoshka wooden toy, you can't miss a chance to visit the Matryoshka factory where professional artisans will show you the whole process of creating the toys. After the excursion you will become an artist and paint your own Matryoshka.
